text: If you're hoping for a repeat of that clear 70-degree start, don't get used to it, the clouds and humidity are already rolling back in. Expect a partly sunny high near 78 today, but showers are likely between 11 and 1, then another round of showers and storms arrives late afternoon into evening. NWS has it at a coin-flip chance, but the European model is much wetter, near 95 percent, so I'd keep the rain jacket handy rather than trust the lighter numbers. Wind is the bigger story on the water: a Lake Wind Advisory is up with south gusts near 30 knots and waves building to 3 to 4 feet, so if you're headed out on the broad waters, get off by early afternoon before things get sloppy. A trace of wildfire smoke may nudge air quality slightly, but it's nothing to worry about. Overnight brings a good soaking, quarter to half an inch, with lows near 66. Wednesday improves, mostly sunny and near 81, with just a brief midday shower risk before things dry out for a beautiful end to the week.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-21T11:00:00+00:00): Clear, 70F feels 70F, humidity 56%, wind S 10 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: A chance of rain showers between 11am and 1pm, then a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms between 4pm and 5pm, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Partly sunny, with a high near 78. South wind 15 to 18 mph, with gusts as high as 35 mph. Chance of precipitation is 50%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.
  Tonight: Showers and thunderstorms. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 66. South wind 12 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 30 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.
  Wednesday: A chance of rain showers between 10am and noon, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms between noon and 1pm, then a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms between 1pm and 2pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 81. Southwest wind around 13 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Wednesday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 57. West wind 6 to 13 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-21T11:12:00+00:00):
  1. Multiple rounds of showers and storms today and tomorrow.
  2. Blue sky conditions to end the week with cool and crisp air.
  3. Quiet and hot weather is expected this weekend before another storm system brings showers and thunderstorms to the region early next week.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 249 AM EDT Tuesday... No significant changes.
LAKE (broad waters):
  ..A Lake Wind Advisory Is In Effect: 
  Today: South winds to 30 knots. Partly sunny. A slight chance of showers in the morning, then a chance of showers with a slight chance of thunderstorms in the afternoon. Waves 2 to 4 feet.
  Tonight: South winds to 30 knots, diminishing to 15 to 25 knots. Becoming partly cloudy overnight. Showers likely with a slight chance of thunderstorms in the evening, then a chance of showers with a slight chance of thunderstorms overnight. Waves 1 to 3 feet, building to 2 to 4 feet, then subsiding to 1 to 3 feet.
  Wednesday: South winds 15 to 20 knots, becoming west 10 to 15 knots. Partly sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
LAKE GAGE: water 71F, level 95.88 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-21: highs [GFS 80, Euro 77, ICON 77] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 65%, Euro 95%, ICON 83%]
  2026-07-22: highs [GFS 79, Euro 78, ICON 74] spread 5F; precip chance [GFS 23%, Euro 91%, ICON 48%]
  2026-07-23: highs [GFS 75, Euro 76, ICON 77] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 9%, Euro 14%, ICON 0%]
AIR: AQI 36 Good (OZONE) — On Tuesday, July 21st, expect a chance for some low-moderate fine particulate matter pollution if a trace of wildfire smoke mixes into the area from the south.
SUN: rise 2026-07-21T05:28-04:00, set 2026-07-21T20:30-04:00, UV max 4.4
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 80, low 67: Chance of afternoon showers. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 60s.
  high 76, low 56: Partly cloudy. Highs in the mid 70s and lows in the mid 50s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: BURLINGTON, Vt. (WCAX) -Happy Tuesday. Clouds came back in through the night and we’ll hang onto that cloud cover through Tuesday. There are a few spotty showers here and there through the first half of the day, but the better chance comes into this afternoon and evening with a few thunderstorms embedded in there as well. Right now, much of our area is under a level 1 out of 5 risk for severe weather, which simply means that an isolated storm or two could be strong to severe in nature with the main threat being gusty winds. If I had to pick an area, it would be across Northern New York, with the storms weakening as they make their arrival into Vermont. High temperatures for the day are a bit cooler, as most climb back into the mid and upper 70s this afternoon. Overnight lows fall into the 60s.
